overview: Izzet Spellementals is a fast, aggressive tempo deck that leverages cheap, high-impact spells and token generation to overwhelm opponents before they can stabilize. By cycling through Traumatic Critique for early damage and using Eddymurk Crab to flood the board with haste elementals, the deck maintains constant pressure. Hearth Elemental provides crucial graveyard recursion to replay key threats and burn spells, extending the game's reach when needed.
win_conditions: Overwhelm the opponent with a wide board of haste elementals and direct damage
Recycle key threats and burn spells via Hearth Elemental to close out games
Maintain tempo advantage by trading efficiently while keeping the board active

keep_criteria: Hands with at least two lands and a one- or two-drop threat
Hands containing Traumatic Critique paired with early board presence
Hands that can play a spell on turn one and follow up on turn two
Keep hands with 2-3 lands (ensuring at least one U and one R source), 3-4 spells (instants/sorceries), and at least one 2-drop spellmentals creature.
Prioritize hands with 1-mana cantrips or cheap removal to stabilize.
mulligan_criteria: Hands with three or more lands
Hands lacking any one- or two-drops
Hands with only high-cost cards and no early interaction
Mulligan hands with 3+ lands, no early spells, or only high-cost threats without fuel.

early_game: Play lands and cheap threats like Eddymurk Crab to establish board presence
Use Traumatic Critique to chip away at the opponent's life total or force unfavorable trades
Prioritize keeping the board active and applying immediate pressure
Turn 1: Play a land, optionally cast a 1-mana cantrip or cheap spell if mana allows.
Turn 2: Play a second land, cast a 1-2 mana spellmentals creature (e.g., Eddymurk Crab), and immediately follow up with an instant/sorcery to trigger its growth ability.
Sequence lands to guarantee U/R availability
if opening with a single color source, consider mulliganing unless you have a cantrip to fix mana.
mid_game: Leverage Hearth Elemental to recycle key spells from the graveyard
Generate additional elementals to widen the board and force blockers
Maintain card advantage by replaying efficient threats and burn spells
Balance mana between casting spells for triggers and developing threats.
Avoid emptying your hand unless you have a clear path to victory.
Use the graveyard as a resource for recursion or alternative wins, but prioritize keeping key interaction in hand.
Track your spell count to maximize spellmentals growth.
Commit threats when you have removal or counterspells to protect them from sweepers or removal.
Hold back if the opponent has unanswered interaction or is building a wide board.
late_game: Use recycled threats to clear the board and close out the game
Push for lethal with a combination of board presence and direct damage
Avoid overcommitting to the board if the opponent has sweepers or board wipes
Large spellmentals with trample or flying, fueled by a full graveyard and hand of cheap spells.
Use cheap instants/sorceries to close out games, either through combat or direct damage.
If the opponent is low on interaction, go wide with pumped threats.
Go all-in when you have card advantage, protection (counters/removal), and the opponent is low on interaction.
Commit when you can trade efficiently or when the opponent is forced to use their last interaction on a non-critical threat.

key_cards.name: Traumatic Critique
key_cards.role: Early-game direct damage and tempo tool
key_cards.notes: Use to chip away at life totals, force unfavorable trades, or finish off opponents when combined with board presence

key_cards.name: Eddymurk Crab
key_cards.role: Token generator and early board presence
key_cards.notes: Play early to flood the board with haste elementals, applying immediate pressure and forcing blockers

key_cards.name: Hearth Elemental
key_cards.role: Graveyard recursion and card advantage engine
key_cards.notes: Use to replay key threats and burn spells from the graveyard, extending the deck's reach and maintaining tempo

sequencing_tips: Hold up Traumatic Critique for instant-speed interaction when possible
Play Eddymurk Crab before Hearth Elemental to maximize token generation and board presence
Recycle high-impact spells first, then use Hearth Elemental to replay threats
Avoid overcommitting to the board if the opponent has sweepers or board wipes
Common mistakes include overcommitting without protection, mismanaging mana for spells vs. creatures, and ignoring graveyard synergy.
Unique interactions include stack manipulation to trigger multiple spellmentals, and using cheap spells to out-value counterspells.
Always track the opponent's interaction and graveyard to maximize your spell-driven triggers.

matchups.advice: Prioritize early board presence and use Traumatic Critique to force unfavorable trades
Apply constant pressure and use Hearth Elemental to bypass card advantage strategies
Race before they stabilize and use recursion to outvalue their resource engine

Use cheap spells to out-value counters, split your threats across multiple turns, and cast multiple spells in response to force counter trade-offs. If available, use uncounterable effects or flashback/graveyard recursion to bypass counters.

Apply early pressure to punish their slow start. Use targeted removal on key ramp pieces (e.g., mana dorks, landfall enablers) and avoid overcommitting to a race. Stabilize with removal/counters, then transition to midrange grind.

Win the spell war by tracking counterspells and prioritizing card advantage. Use targeted removal efficiently, and don't overcommit to the board. Force the opponent to use interaction on non-critical threats, then close out with a pumped spellmentals.

common_threats: Fast sweepers that clear the board
Card draw engines that outpace recursion
Life gain effects that extend the game
Watch for sweepers (e.g., board wipes), counterspells, and fast aggro threats.
Note when the opponent saves removal vs. when they commit to a sweeper.
Track their graveyard if recursion is a threat, and monitor their mana for key combo pieces or finishers.
